_doc: Sprint 13 / S13.2.3 — recorded `trivy fs --format=json` output. Two Trivy `Results` blocks (one per target file): a Dockerfile referencing ubuntu:14.04 (CRITICAL EOL) and a requirements.txt pinning requests==2.6.0 (HIGH CVE-2018-18074). Plus an UNKNOWN-severity finding to exercise the default-fallback mapping path.

Vulnerabilities.Title: Base image ubuntu:14.04 reached end-of-life in 2019
Vulnerabilities.Description: ubuntu:14.04 no longer receives security updates from Canonical; rebuild on a supported LTS (20.04, 22.04, or 24.04).

Vulnerabilities.Title: Authorization header leaked on cross-origin redirects in requests
Vulnerabilities.Description: The Requests package through 2.19.1 sends an HTTP Authorization header to an http URI upon receiving a same-hostname https-to-http redirect, which makes it easier for remote attackers to discover credentials by sniffing the network.
Synthetic UNKNOWN-severity entry to exercise the default-mapping path.
